Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase with locative postposition; metaphorical agricultural clause
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Investing effort now to gain benefits later
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Aj kam kana, pare phal paba
Usage Contexts: daily speech, family advice, farming discussions
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from traditional Santali agricultural cycles where sowing determines future harvest
First Example Sentence: Baba bolena, serma re bij ropena, tahen kal bhalo din asena.
Second Example Sentence: Skul re pora do serma re bij ropena kana.
Third Example Sentence: Tor aj mehnat serma re bij ropena jaisa.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Refers to the Bonga spirits central to Santali cosmology
First Example Sentence: Exam re pass helena, lagena bonga re ashirbad menk'ena.
Second Example Sentence: Tor jibon re bonga re ashirbad menk'ena lagena.
Third Example Sentence: Baha parab re sab hor bonga re ashirbad menk'ena kahena.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Inspired by moon symbolism common in Santali songs and folklore
First Example Sentence: Tor sahajyo chando menk' re andhar jaha jaisa lagena.
Second Example Sentence: Dukh samay re chando menk' re andhar jaha dekha jaha.
Third Example Sentence: Uni katha sunke chando menk' re andhar jaha lagena.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Possessive noun phrase with metaphorical support structure
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Community strength supported by spiritual belief
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects traditional Santali village solidarity and spiritual worldview
First Example Sentence: Disom re hor hopon re bonga sahara dekha jaha.
Second Example Sentence: Parab samay re hor hopon re bonga sahara majbut lagena.
Third Example Sentence: Adivasi samaj hor hopon re bonga sahara re thik achena.

Idiom (Santali): Dare hor do serma re phul kana
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Sadharan manush bhi bada kam kar sakena
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Nature imagery common in Santali oral storytelling traditions
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Santali): /dare hor do serma re pʰul kana/
First Example Sentence: Uni choto chilen, kintu dare hor do serma re phul kana helena.
Second Example Sentence: Tor kam dekhe lagena dare hor do serma re phul kana.
Third Example Sentence: Gaon re ek chele dare hor do serma re phul kana helena.

Idiom (Santali): Jibon re dhorjo sabse boro sikna
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Patience is the greatest lesson in life
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common wisdom transmitted through Santali oral teaching
First Example Sentence: Baba bolena jibon re dhorjo sabse boro sikna.
Second Example Sentence: Kam re safalta chao to jibon re dhorjo sabse boro sikna mane.
Third Example Sentence: Uni jibon re dhorjo sabse boro sikna bujhena.

Idiom (Santali): Disom re ekta sabse boro shakti
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Sab milke kam kana to shakti badena
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to collective resistance movements in Santali history
First Example Sentence: Netaji bolena disom re ekta sabse boro shakti.
Second Example Sentence: Gaon re sab mile disom re ekta sabse boro shakti dekhaena.
Third Example Sentence: Samaj sudhar re disom re ekta sabse boro shakti darkar.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Village communication patterns where news spreads rapidly
First Example Sentence: Tor khabar sereng re katha hawa jaisa phailena.
Second Example Sentence: Gaon re gossip sereng re katha hawa jaisa jaha.
Third Example Sentence: Uni bolena katha sereng re katha hawa jaisa.

Idiom (Santali): Baha parab re mon phul jaisa
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Baha Parab is a major Santali spring flower festival
First Example Sentence: Aji baha parab re mon phul jaisa lagena.
Second Example Sentence: Nach gana dekhe mon baha parab re mon phul jaisa helena.
Third Example Sentence: Sab hor baha parab re mon phul jaisa hasena.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Bring hope or guidance in dark times
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Light vs darkness metaphor common in Santali folklore
First Example Sentence: Tor sahajyo andhar re bati jala jaisa lagena.
Second Example Sentence: Guru bolena andhar re bati jala darkar.
Third Example Sentence: Uni gaon re andhar re bati jala helena.
